description_long: This is a large, multi-winged school building with a symmetrical layout and a prominent central section featuring a tower-like structure. The exterior is primarily composed of red brick with gray stone or concrete accents, and the roof is pitched with a combination of gabled and hipped sections. The building has numerous windows arranged in regular rows, suggesting multiple classrooms and administrative spaces. Architectural details include decorative cornices, arched entryways, and a central spire or cupola that adds vertical emphasis. The structure appears institutional and historic, reminiscent of late 19th or early 20th-century educational architecture. The overall impression is of a substantial, formal, and enduring public school building.
description: A large, red-brick school building with a central tower, pitched roofs, and symmetrical wings, featuring historic architectural details and numerous windows.
description_view_0: This view highlights the side and partial front of the school, showing the central tower and the intersection of two main wings.
description_view_1: The front facade is visible, with the main entrance, central tower, and symmetrical wings clearly displayed.
description_view_2: The rear and side of the building are shown, emphasizing the back wing and the continuation of the pitched roof design.
description_view_3: The opposite side and rear are visible, with the central tower and the back section of the building prominent.
